purpose: Transcript availability probe for a YouTube Shorts fragrance rhetorical-tone fixture spike.
probe_notes: The initial caption batch had one failed row because video_id -V7MN2IWMpA begins with a dash; rerun with --video-id=-V7MN2IWMpA succeeded.
Caption packets canonicalize locator URLs to /watch?v=...; Shorts identity is inherited from the candidate pool serving-surface verification, not from transcript packets.
ASR fallback wrote raw audio packets and derived transcript_asr records under the temporary data root only.
Two ASR fallbacks produced explicit no_speech records with zero cues; these are successful captures but not text-bearing transcripts.
non_claims: not tone labeling or tone scoring
not a claim that captions are clean human transcripts
not media preservation for caption-captured rows
not comment capture or engagement-context capture
raw transcript/audio packets remain in C:\tmp and are not committed to the repository
